_comment: Round-248 bilingual fixtures for Auth. Used by auth_describe_challenge.sh + challenges/runner/main.go to verify UTF-8 round-trip across JWT claims and API key metadata. CONST-046 alignment: i18n.Translator NoopTranslator returns bundle keys verbatim — these fixtures prove that opaque arg values (Cyrillic, Japanese, Arabic, Han) survive the full create/validate/store/retrieve cycle byte-for-byte. Article XI §11.9 forensic anchor.

subjects.name: 田中
subjects.key_label: 本番読み取り専用APIキー

expected_invariants.jwt_claim_byte_preservation: Every claim value passed to jwt.Manager.Create MUST round-trip via Validate byte-for-byte — including Cyrillic, Han, Arabic, and CJK characters.
expected_invariants.apikey_name_byte_preservation: apikey.Generator.Generate + InMemoryStore.Store/Get MUST preserve the Name field byte-for-byte across non-ASCII locales.
expected_invariants.scopes_preservation: Non-ASCII scope strings MUST be preserved through HasScope / HasAllScopes lookups (byte-equality semantics).
expected_invariants.i18n_passthrough: i18n.NoopTranslator.T(key, arg) MUST emit key + 0x1F + arg verbatim — no encoding mutation, no case-fold, no normalization. This is the load-bearing guarantee that consuming projects' real translators receive byte-clean arguments.
expected_invariants.refresh_preserves_custom_claims: jwt.Manager.Refresh MUST preserve every non-standard claim verbatim (standard claims exp/iat/iss are regenerated).
